mission_title: AuraAI Mission Zero — Building an AI Company in Public
objective: Create the first public YouTube video introducing AuraAI, why it was built, what currently exists, how founder control works, and what the audience can expect from the journey.
topic: I built an AI media company with specialized AI employees
target_audience: AI enthusiasts, developers, entrepreneurs, creators, automation builders, and people interested in building companies with AI agents.
audience_problem: Most AI-agent content shows isolated demos or exaggerated autonomous systems without explaining the real architecture, limitations, safeguards, failures, and engineering process.
audience_promise: Give viewers an honest behind-the-scenes introduction to AuraAI and show how a founder-controlled AI company is being built step by step.
content_goal: Introduce the AuraAI brand, build trust, earn subscriptions, and begin the Building AuraAI in Public series.
tone: Authentic, professional, educational, ambitious, transparent, and free of exaggerated hype.
primary_call_to_action: Subscribe and follow the journey as AuraAI develops from a local project into a functioning AI media company.
source_notes: AuraAI is a founder-controlled AI media operating system built in Python.

It includes mission execution, specialized AI employee roles, provider routing, deterministic fallback, Gemini integration, production planning, Creative Quality review, rendering foundations, distribution, analytics, runtime state, and a local dashboard.
The project currently has 360 passing automated tests.
Gemini live connectivity was verified through a founder-approved smoke test.
Content missions stop at founder review unless explicitly approved.
Rendering and publishing remain separately controlled.
AuraAI currently has public accounts on YouTube, Instagram, X, and TikTok.
source_references: Founder-supplied notes in this mission input.
Founder-supplied local test results and Git history.
No external market or trend research was performed or supplied.
constraints: Do not claim AuraAI is fully autonomous.
Do not claim AuraAI is profitable.
Do not claim AuraAI automatically publishes content.
Do not claim that AI employees are human employees.
Clearly explain that founder approval remains required.
Do not invent statistics, users, revenue, customers, or results.
Keep technical explanations understandable to non-developers.
Do not claim external market or trend research occurred.
